summary: Iteration 3 full coverage: 18/18 new backend pytest cases pass (notifications CRUD + all 5 trigger types, file uploads with scope authZ, 413 over-size, message file attach + invalid-id 400, reviews gated on completed contracts with dup-block + rating recompute, dispute open/release/refund flows with admin authZ, dispute.resolved notifications to both parties). Frontend UI smoke: alice sign-in works, notifications-trigger + badge (5 unread) render, notifications-panel opens and fetches, attach-file button present on /messages, admin login → Disputes tab lists disputes and shows release/refund resolutions.

minor.issue: Previously-reported 500 is now fixed — verified endpoint returns 200 with payment_status + cached fallback per test_worksoy_iter3 prerequisites.

ui_bugs.issue: Panel shows 'Loading…' briefly then renders items — no visible spinner/skeleton, fine, but no empty-state message when 0 items.

Tab label says 'Disputes (0)' even though 2 resolved disputes are listed below. Counter appears to be counting only 'open' disputes while the list shows all statuses — either scope the list to open-only or make the counter reflect total.

issues: Third-party analytics (shipper-api-production-3b99) CORS-errors on every page — non-fatal noise, unchanged from prior iteration.

action_items: Fix AdminPage Disputes tab count so it reflects what the list actually renders (either scope list to status='open' or include resolved in the count).
Low-priority: add empty-state text to NotificationsBell panel when there are no notifications (currently renders blank after 'Loading…').
Low-priority (unchanged from prior iteration): ConvexProvider / VITE_CONVEX_URL placeholder still emits a console error on some routes.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py now 1486 LOC — still well past the 700-line guideline. Split into routers (notifications.py, files.py, reviews.py, disputes.py) alongside the prior-iteration split recommendation.

server.py line 1141 (_authorize_file_scope): the ordering `if conversation_id / if contract_id / if milestone_id` is permissive — if the caller passes ALL three, each is authorized independently; that is OK today but if any future scope is added make sure the OR semantics remain correct.

server.py line 1164 (upload_file): writes to disk BEFORE the 25MB check triggers — we do unlink on oversize but partial bytes still hit the filesystem during the streamed write; acceptable with current chunk=1MB but consider checking Content-Length header upfront to reject earlier.

server.py line 1341 (file_dispute): loops `async for a in db.users.find({'role':'admin'})` and inserts a notification per admin — fine for small admin counts but if admins grow, batch insert_many would be cleaner.

server.py line 1412 (refund branch): resets milestone to 'pending' and sets funded_at=None, but does NOT emit a 'refund' payment_transactions record — ledger divergence if the milestone was actually funded via Stripe. Add an audit row so accounting has trail of the refund.
Frontend ContractPage: review-form re-renders even after successful submit — no 'thank you' / read-only confirmation state. Minor UX polish.

success_rate.frontend: 95% — all iter-3 testids render; minor Disputes tab counter mismatch

test_credentials: alice@worksoy.com / Passw0rd!  |  admin@worksoy.com / WorkSoy!Admin2026  |  TEST_iter3_expert_<uuid>@worksoy.com (fresh per run)
seed_data_creation: Each run creates: 1 brief ('TEST iter3 contract …'), 1 proposal, 1 accepted contract driven to 'completed' via direct mongo update, plus 1 'TEST iter3 disp …' brief + contract for the release/refund dispute flows. Also 1 'TEST iter3 reject notif' brief. One review record remains per run. Uploaded test files left under /app/backend/uploads (hello.txt, attach.bin, big.bin auto-unlinked on 413). These accumulate across iterations.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_worksoy_iter3.py -v` for the iteration-3 surface and `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_worksoy_flows.py -v` for the prior regression. Both should pass (53/54 total, the single prior failure was payments/status 500 which is now fixed per this iteration note). Frontend UI tests use the preview URL from /app/frontend/.env (REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL) — the one hard-baked into the Vite build is https://e9dbddd0-8a69-4c37-9baa-eca9f6a0a6f4.preview.emergentagent.com; use that one for browser tests. To force a contract to 'completed' without Stripe: direct mongo update db.milestones.updateMany({contract_id:X},{$set:{status:'released'}}) + db.contracts.updateOne({id:X},{$set:{status:'completed'}}). The iteration-3 test file does this inline via pymongo.
rca_of_the_issue: None — all iter-3 features working. The only iter-3 UI nit is AdminPage Disputes tab label '(0)' not matching the list count; likely a count query that filters status='open' while the list fetches all. Trivial fix in AdminPage.tsx.
